issues: Broken wiki-link [[QMS]] — no matching file or alias (confirmed by test-report.json structural check).
EUDAMED mandatory date 28 May 2026 for four modules confirmed correct by official EC source.

Claim that EUDAMED offers a 'FHIR-basierte REST-API für automatisierte Registrierungen' is unverifiable from official EC sources. Official EC documentation does not describe a FHIR REST API for EUDAMED registration — EUDAMED uses a web portal interface and XML-based data exchange, not FHIR. This claim may be inaccurate.
Osborne Clarke source (law firm blog) used for the primary mandatory date. Acceptable as secondary source but official EC URL is more authoritative.
articles.fixInstructions: 1. Fix the [[QMS]] link: either create a QMS stub article, or replace with plain text 'Qualitätsmanagementsystem (QMS)' and add a note '(ISO 13485)' to maintain usefulness. 2. In the [!interesse-technik] callout, remove or qualify the 'FHIR-basierte REST-API' claim. Replace with: 'EUDAMED-Zugang: webgate.ec.europa.eu/eudamed. Registrierung über das Webportal oder über strukturierten Datenaustausch gemäß EUDAMED Technical Onboarding Guide.' 3. Add official EC source to Quellen: https://health.ec.europa.eu/latest-updates/eudamed-four-first-modules-will-be-mandatory-use-28-may-2026-2025-11-27_en
